cassette: none: reducer.PostgresMultiCloudRuntimeDriftWriter operates over []model.Candidate Go values, not a CanonicalMaterialization; the two fixture candidates live inline in go/internal/replay/costcounting/multi_cloud_runtime_drift_cost_test.go
description: Two admitted candidates with distinct canonical cloud_resource_uid identity (one orphaned GCP compute instance, one unmanaged Azure storage account) in one scope, driven through the production Postgres fact writer over an InstrumentedDB-wrapped counting fake. WriteMultiCloudRuntimeDriftFindings issues ONE ExecContext PER candidate in a single loop — there is no reducerBatchInsertFacts call anywhere in this writer, exactly like its sibling aws_cloud_runtime_drift writer — so this writer's Postgres write cost is O(N) round-trips per candidate list: two candidates cost exactly eshu_dp_postgres_query_duration_seconds=2 write observations and statements_executed=2. This budget is asserted EXACT (==, not <=): it encodes the CURRENT known per-row write amplification rather than absorbing it silently. Migrating this writer onto the shared batched bulk-insert path is a follow-on tracked separately (C-14 issue #4367 orchestration); until then, any deviation from exactly 2 trips this gate. The standard N+1 negative control (call once per row vs. once for the whole batch) is a structural no-op for this per-row writer, mirroring the confirmed aws_cloud_runtime_drift and package_source_correlation precedent, so it is deferred to the same follow-on rather than asserted here as a false negative control.
refresh_path: manual: edit the inline fixture candidates in multi_cloud_runtime_drift_cost_test.go and this file together, reviewed in the same diff (no credentialed cassette refresh applies since there is no cassette)
